channel.title: LLM Vibes Research
channel.description: Independent analysis of AI model quality and incidents from the LLM Vibes data set.

item.title: Claude Code lost 19 points during the cache bug. The rest of Claude lost 4.
item.description: Aggregate model scores treat each frontier model as one product. Sliced by product surface across 60 days of public posts, Claude Code and ChatGPT.com are where the conversation actually happens, and the same model's scores diverge sharply by surface during a regression.

item.title: We Caught Claude's March Slide 28 Days Before Anthropic Confirmed It
item.description: Independent sentiment data caught Claude Code grumbling on March 26, the day Anthropic shipped the cache bug. 28 days before the postmortem.

item.title: How LLM Vibes Classifies Sentiment
item.description: The full pipeline from scraper to score. Five platforms, 12 complaint categories, a volume-weighted 0–100 score, and the failure modes we've documented but not yet solved.

item.title: When One AI Cracks: Cross-Model Sentiment, March–April 2026
item.description: Comparing absolute scores or even bug-window deltas across LLM Vibes models can mislead you. What actually caught Claude's March 2026 regression was the post-fix recovery shape: ChatGPT recovered, Gemini stayed flat, Claude kept sliding.
